message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Ristorante Mamma Gina (Palm Desert)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 1,463 reviews; Tripadvisor 1,146 reviews — combined 2,500+ across major platforms
- Sentiment: mostly positive
- Longevity: Operating in Palm Desert since September 1986 (approximately 40 years); founded by Piero Pierattoni; family-owned and operated; sister to Trattoria Mamma Gina in Florence (1956 origin per own site). Long staff tenure cited on own About page (sous chefs 27 years, most employees 15+ years) [UNVERIFIED — sourced only to own site]
- Credentials: ["Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice (ranked #7 of ~247-296 Palm Desert restaurants at 4.3/5)","Recognition in Palm Springs Life Magazine reader 'Best of the Best' Italian category [UNVERIFIED — surfaced in search summary but no direct primary-source citation found in this audit]","Featured in Locale Magazine 'Best Italian Restaurants in Palm Desert' editorial"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for 'best Italian restaurants Palm Desert', 'date night Palm Desert', and 'El Paseo Italian'. Appears in Locale Magazine, Tripadvisor, OpenTable, Wanderlog, and visitgreaterpalmsprings.com listings. Does NOT surface prominently in broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'best fine dining in the desert' agent answers (Palm Springs Life's '8 Best Fine Dining' list and AvantStay's '18 Best Restaurants in Coachella Valley' do not lead with it). Weaker visibility in 'Tuscan near Palm Springs' or 'Florentine cuisine Coachella Valley' regional queries where Pinzimini (with James Beard-honored chef) often ranks higher in agent summaries.
- Site structured data: Weak. mammagina.com carries a single JSON-LD block typed as LocalBusiness (not Restaurant), with an empty addressCountry, no streetAddress beyond the business name placeholder, no openingHoursSpecification, no servesCuisine, no menu URL, no telephone, no priceRange. Geo coordinates are present. Hours are human-readable on the page but not machine-structured. Menu pages exist but lack Menu/MenuSection/MenuItem schema. Agent-readability is poor relative to a 40-year institution.
- Visibility gap score: 7
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify these claims through independent searches.
Acknowledged. Now proceeding with verification searches.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Ristorante Mamma Gina in Palm Desert:

**History and Origins:**
Ristorante Mamma Gina has been a landmark in Florentine cuisine in Italy since 1956. Thirty years later, in 1986, California residents were able to get their very own taste of Italy as Ristorante Mamma Gina made its way to the United States.

**Piero Pierattoni:**
Piero Pierattoni has been in the restaurant business in Florence, Italy since he was 15 years old, and his family owned Ristorante Mamma Gina in Florence. A native of Florence, Piero moved from Italy to the desert in 1986 and opened Mamma Gina that same year.

**Establishment in Palm Desert:**
The restaurant opened in September 1986 on beautiful El Paseo Drive in Palm Desert, Ca. The consistency and the quality of food and service has allowed the restaurant to continue years after years and incredible success rarely achievable in this part of the world.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Mamma Gina and its coverage in Locale Magazine:

## Ristorante Mamma Gina in Palm Desert

Ristorante Mamma Gina is a historic eatery in the heart of Palm Desert that has been serving local desert patrons since 1986. Founded in 1986, Mamma Gina brought over its Italian heritage from Florence to serve the residents of Palm Springs with authentic and traditional Italian cuisine.

## Locale Magazine Coverage

Locale Magazine has featured Ristorante Mamma Gina extensively as one of the best Italian restaurants in the area. The Palm Desert restaurant features a sophisticated yet relaxed ambiance, with white tablecloths and flowing drapes, making it the perfect spot for an intimate dinner or date night.

The publication highlighted several signature dishes, including:
- The Fettuccine ai Funghi, which features egg fettuccine tossed with porcini mushrooms and bathed in a rich truffle butter sauce.
- Mezzelune all'Aragosta, which features handmade black squid ink ravioli stuffed with fresh ricotta and succulent lobster meat.

The restaurant also has one of the best happy hours in the desert, which can be enjoyed seven days a week from 4 p.m. to close.

The restaurant is located at 73705 El Paseo, Palm Desert, CA 92260.

input.prompt: What awards, press, or recognition does the About page claim? Mention any Palm Springs Life, Locale Magazine, James Beard, Food Network, or other awards listed. Also note staff tenure claims.

content.content:
# Awards and Recognition

The About page does **not claim any specific awards, press mentions, or recognition** from publications like Palm Springs Life, Locale Magazine, James Beard, or Food Network.

## Staff Tenure Claims

The page does include the following staffing claims:

- "Our sous chefs have been in the family business for the past 27 years"
- "all of our employees have been with us no less than 15 years"

I found several results from Palm Springs Life regarding their "Best of the Best" winners for Italian restaurants. Here are the Italian restaurants that have won in their Best of the Best competition:

**Recent Winners (2025):**

The winners for Best Italian Cuisine include restaurants such as:

- Johnny Costa's Ristorante in Palm Springs
- A lively eatery at Civic Center Plaza that specializes in hearty dishes like lasagna Bolognese, gnocchi, and pizza
- Norma's, where traditional pastas, seafood, and meat dishes pair with sandwiches, pizzas, and house wine
- A family-owned spot that has been serving up Italian fare like osso bucco and filetto Marsala for more than three decades (Castelli's)
- Enzo's bistro-style restaurant in Old Town La Quinta with housemade pasta and sauces, popular for Calabrese-style lasagna and eggplant Parmigiano stack

Johnny Costa's has been honored by Palm Springs Life readers as the city's Best Italian Restaurant for 13 consecutive years (and 14 consecutive years according to another source).

You can find the complete 2025 list by visiting the Palm Springs Life website at palmspringslife.com, as the full list of 2025 winners across all 29 categories is available there.

Based on the search results, I found information about Ristorante Mamma Gina and its recognition:

Ristorante Mamma Gina is voted yearly as the "Best of the Best Italian Restaurant" in Palm Desert. The restaurant continues to serve authentic homemade pasta and Tuscan cuisine, offering dishes like honey-truffle chicken Milanese and other Mediterranean delights.

Ristorante Mamma Gina has been a landmark in Florentine cuisine in Italy since 1956, and expanded to California in 1986. The family business was established in Florence, Italy, and later grew to include an elegant location on El Paseo.

The restaurant has received this "Best of the Best" recognition from Palm Springs Life magazine and other local sources highlighting Italian restaurants in the Palm Desert area.

I've found information about Ristorante Mamma Gina in Palm Desert. Here's what the search revealed:

Ristorante Mamma Gina is an Italian restaurant in Palm Desert that has been operating for 39 years. The restaurant's owner Piero Pierattoni was in the restaurant business in Florence, Italy since age 15, his family owned Ristorante Mamma Gina in Florence, and the Palm Desert location opened in September 1986.

For 40 years, Ristorante Mamma Gina has been serving authentic Tuscan dishes to the Palm Desert community. On Tripadvisor, it is rated 4.3 of 5 and ranked #8 of 241 restaurants in Palm Desert.

However, regarding the Michelin guide specifically, the search results do not contain any information about whether Ristorante Mamma Gina has received a Michelin star or recognition. If you're interested in learning about the restaurant's Michelin status, you may want to check the official Michelin Guide website directly.

content.text:
I have enough to render judgment. Summary:

- 1986 founding by Piero Pierattoni — confirmed via multiple independent sources.
- Florence 1956 sister origin — confirmed broadly.
- Tripadvisor metrics (1,146 reviews, 4.3, ranked #7 of 247, Travelers' Choice badge) — confirmed via direct fetch.
- Yelp 1,463 reviews — supported by Yelp listing title in search results (Updated June 2026 - 937 Photos & 1463 Reviews).
- Locale Magazine feature — confirmed.
- Palm Springs Life "Best of the Best" — UNVERIFIED to refuted. The 2025 Best of the Best Italian winners are Johnny Costa's, Castelli's, Enzo's, Norma's per PSL — Mamma Gina is not in that list. Johnny Costa's has won 13-14 consecutive years. The "voted yearly Best of the Best Italian Restaurant" line appears to come from the restaurant's own marketing copy or aggregator description. Cannot confirm an actual PSL Reader's Choice win.
- Staff tenure claims — only sourced to own About page, no independent verification.
- No James Beard, Michelin, or Food Network claims were made (correctly absent).

confirms: Operating in Palm Desert since September 1986 — confirmed by multiple independent sources (El Paseo Catalogue, Locale Magazine, restaurateur.com, LinkedIn)
Founded by Piero Pierattoni, a Florence native who moved to the desert in 1986 — confirmed
Sister/parent restaurant in Florence, Italy dating to 1956 — confirmed across multiple independent listings
Family-owned and operated — confirmed across independent sources
Tripadvisor metrics: 1,146 reviews, 4.3/5, ranked #7 of 247 Palm Desert restaurants — directly confirmed via Tripadvisor page fetch
Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice award — directly confirmed on the Tripadvisor listing badge
Yelp 1,463 reviews — confirmed in Yelp page title (Updated June 2026 - 1463 Reviews)
Featured in Locale Magazine 'Best Italian Restaurants in Palm Desert' editorial — confirmed at localemagazine.com/best-italian-restaurants-in-palm-desert/
Listed on visitgreaterpalmsprings.com and Discover Palm Desert dining guides — confirmed
contradicts: Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' Italian recognition is highly questionable. Palm Springs Life's actual 'Best of the Best 2025' Italian category winners are Johnny Costa's, Castelli's, Enzo's, and Norma's — Mamma Gina is NOT on that list. Johnny Costa's has held the reader-voted Best Italian title for 13-14 consecutive years per PSL coverage. The 'voted yearly Best of the Best' phrasing surfacing in search summaries traces to the restaurant's own marketing copy / aggregator pages, not a PSL Reader's Choice win. This claim should be removed or significantly downgraded.
flagged_as_unverified: Long staff tenure claims (sous chefs 27 years, employees 15+ years) — only sourced to the restaurant's own About page; no independent corroboration found
Approximately 40 years of operation — arithmetically consistent (1986-2026 = 40 years) but rounding from 39 years; minor
input.notes: Core history (1986 Palm Desert founding, Pierattoni ownership, 1956 Florence lineage) is solid and well-corroborated. Tripadvisor metrics and Travelers' Choice are confirmed by direct fetch. The weak link is the Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' Italian claim — Palm Springs Life's actual Reader's Choice Italian winners are a different set of restaurants (Johnny Costa's has dominated for over a decade). The brief already flagged this as unverified; evidence leans toward refutation, so recommend removing it from the credentials list. No James Beard, Michelin, or Food Network claims were made (appropriately absent — none exist).
